attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AMS casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age (inferred ~55) and sex_at_birth (inferred female) are NOT stated in the source note — required schema fields, inferred conservatively for an adult with pemphigus foliaceus; clinician to confirm.
Geographic context (RA1_major_city) inferred (private residence, no location detail in source).
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-03-00235 — SEQ is provisional (derived from source AMS-035 into the 002xx range); maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-03 bucket.

difficulty_tier set to red_herring_laden (DD ordinal 03): the case is built around dangerous mimics (eczema herpeticum, severe impetigo, Stevens-Johnson syndrome) and a chronic seborrhoeic-dermatitis mislabel.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the in-person discriminators (positive Nikolsky sign, professional mucosal inspection, crust-morphology assessment, absence of bullae/lymphadenopathy, measured vitals) were sealed into 10/11 as clinician-only findings; patient-reportable negatives (no fever, no mouth/eye involvement) and video-observable skin appearance placed in 01. Baseline tier set to T3 (urgent same-day in-person exam) because the AI cannot remotely exclude the dangerous mimics — not T5 (no life-threat) and not routine (would risk missing a mimic).

Topical corticosteroid strengths (hydrocortisone 1% / betamethasone valerate 0.02%) are taken verbatim from the source; exact application frequency and duration were not specified in the source and were left general (not invented).
Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes for the primary diagnosis and differentials are best-effort and UNVERIFIED pending terminology receipt; eczema herpeticum code in particular should be confirmed.
No AMT codes assigned for the topical corticosteroid/emollient because a specific product/strength AMT concept was not confidently known — flagged rather than guessed.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
